task_completion.description: Launch Bridge Command and select the scenario 'z) Traffic Safety Assessment'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bridge Command application is open and the scenario 'z) Traffic Safety Assessment' is selected from the menu or actively running.

task_completion.description: Identify the three vessels in range using the radar
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bridge Command radar interface is visible, showing three distinct targets/vessels being tracked or queried for information.

task_completion.description: Create the output file with the correct header
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application is open showing a file saved as '/home/ga/Documents/traffic_log.csv' with the exact first line 'Name,CPA,TCPA'.

task_completion.description: Record the names of the three vessels in the CSV file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CSV file contains rows for the three vessels: 'MV Sky', 'Tanker Blue', and 'FV Horizon'.

task_completion.description: Record the CPA and TCPA values for all targets
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CSV file contains numerical values for CPA and TCPA next to each vessel name. MV Sky should be ~0.0 nm / ~9.0 min, Tanker Blue should be ~0.5 nm / ~9.3 min, and FV Horizon should be ~0.76 nm / ~0 min (within tolerances).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Bridge Command application to gather data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Bridge Command GUI and radar screen being actively used, not just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent manually recorded the data or used standard GUI tools rather than running a hidden script to generate the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing the values into a text editor/spreadsheet or using standard copy-paste from the application UI.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the values from the radar interface rather than guessing or hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks on or tracks the targets in the Bridge Command radar interface to reveal their CPA/TCPA values before writing them down.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Scenario name: 'z) Traffic Safety Assessment'. Output file: '/home/ga/Documents/traffic_log.csv'. Expected header: 'Name,CPA,TCPA'. The three vessels are 'MV Sky' (CPA ~0.0 nm, TCPA ~9.0 min), 'Tanker Blue' (CPA ~0.4977 nm, TCPA ~9.31 min), and 'FV Horizon' (CPA ~0.7617 nm, TCPA ~0 min). Tolerances are +/- 0.5 nm for CPA and +/- 5.0 min for TCPA.
